title: App CSS
content:
This file contains the global CSS styles for the DocMed application. It defines the color palette, typography, layout structures, and styling for various UI components. **Purpose:** To provide a consistent and themable styling foundation for the DocMed application, ensuring a coherent user experience across different components and views. **Key Features & Styling Areas:** * **Reset & Base Styles:** Includes a CSS reset and base styles for the `body` element, setting up font family, background, text color, and basic layout properties. * **Color Palette & Variables:** Defines CSS variables (`--bg`, `--surface`, `--text`, `--accent`, etc.) for easy theming and color management. * **Layout:** Styles for the main application layout (`.layout`, `.main-column`), establishing a flexbox-based structure. * **Tab Bar:** Styling for the tab navigation component (`.tab-bar`, `.tab-list`, `.tab`, `.tab-add`), including active states and hover effects. * **Sidebar:** Styles for the collapsible sidebar (`.sidebar`, `.sidebar.collapsed`, `.sidebar-top`, `.sidebar-body`), including collapse button and repository title styling. * **Search Input:** Styling for the search component (`.search-wrap`, `.search-input`, `.search-clear`). * **Navigation:** Styles for the navigation tree (`.nav`, `.nav-item`, `.nav-section-label`), including active states, hover effects, and badges. * **File Tree:** Styles for the file tree component (`.tree-level`, `.tree-row`, `.tree-folder-row`, `.tree-chevron`, `.tree-name`, `.tree-children`), handling folder expansion and item selection. * **Main Content Area:** Styles for the main content display (`.main`, `.page`), including page titles and subtitles. * **Stats Cards:** Styling for displaying statistics (`.stats-row`, `.stat-card`, `.stat-value`, `.stat-label`). * **Home Page Sections:** Styles for organizing content on the home page, such as file grids (`.file-grid`, `.file-card`). * **Commit History:** Styling for displaying commit information (`.commit-list`, `.commit-item`, `.commit-sha`, `.commit-msg`, `.commit-meta`). * **Doc Page Specifics:** Styles for the file path display on documentation pages (`.doc-filepath`). * **Markdown Rendering:** Styles for rendering markdown content (`.markdown-body`), including headings, paragraphs, links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, and tables, ensuring they integrate with the application's theme. * **Utility Styles:** Includes styles for empty states (`.empty-state`) and loading animations (`.loading-pulse`). **Usage:** This CSS file is typically imported into the main application entry point (e.g., `App.tsx` or `index.tsx`) to apply the global styles. **Example (Conceptual Import):** ```jsx // src/App.tsx import React from 'react'; import './App.css'; // Import the global styles function App() { return (
{/* Sidebar content */}
{/* Tab bar content */}
{/* Main page content */}
); } export default App; ```
